description: Lap 1 rewrites seed/rfc.md to add the missing Decision and Consequences sections, consistent with the Status (Accepted) and the three options already laid out in Context. check_rfc.py exits 0 on lap 1. Demonstrates the canonical loop reaching DONE with zero API keys and zero external tools.

interactions.agent_output: Ran check_rfc.py: 2 sections missing — Decision and Consequences. The Status is already 'Accepted' and Context lists three options (keep shell script, static site generator, fold into Next.js portal). Adding a Decision section choosing the static site generator option and a Consequences section stating the real tradeoffs. Not touching the checker.

actions.content:
# RFC-014: Move newsletter build to a static site generator

## Status

Accepted

## Context

The newsletter is currently hand-assembled via a shell script that
concatenates HTML fragments. Each issue takes 20+ minutes to build and the
marker-based templating (`build-html.sh`) is fragile — a renamed section
heading silently breaks the build with no error, which has caused at least
two late-night re-sends.

We evaluated three options: keep the shell script and add tests, move to a
static site generator (Eleventy/Astro), or move the whole newsletter into
the existing Next.js portal codebase.

## Decision

Move the newsletter build to a static site generator (Astro), separate from
both the shell script and the Next.js portal. Astro's content-collections
give us schema-validated frontmatter (so a renamed field fails the build
loudly instead of silently), and keeping it out of the portal avoids
coupling the newsletter's release cadence to the portal's deploy pipeline.

## Consequences

- The existing `build-html.sh` marker-based templating is retired; issues
  8-10 need a one-time migration to Astro content files.
- Build time drops from 20+ minutes to under a minute, and a broken
  heading/marker now fails the build instead of silently shipping.
- The newsletter gets its own deploy target, so a portal deploy can no
  longer accidentally break newsletter issue rendering.
- Adds one more deploy target to maintain (Astro site alongside the
  portal), which is an acceptable tradeoff given the fragility it removes.

interactions.notes: The fix — check_rfc.py now exits 0. bl run reaches DONE on lap 1, keyless, no external tool.

interactions.agent_output: Fix already applied; no further changes needed.
interactions.notes: Fallback for any lap beyond 1 — should not be reached in the happy path.
